reviews.review_text:
It says I've primarily played on the Steam Deck, that's not true - I play with a controller on my custom PC with Steam OS.

The gameplay is phenominal, it is the perfect modern Budokai Tenkaichi gameplay. However, there is not enough content to justify the regular price. All the DLC there is should have been included in the base game, and even if it were it still would not be enough content to justify the price point. Honestly, they should have just made Dragon Ball Z: Kakarot with this specific fighting gameplay and it would have been the perfect Budokai Tenkaichi game.

The major issue is the online community. The few players that are left use the most annoying, most cheesy, and most toxic strategies using broken mechanics just to win the match. They would rather prevent you from playing just to get that sweet, sweet W. This game is only worth playing multiplayer with friends, because these players have basically annoyed everyone else off of it.

For the little single player content there is, it's only really worth getting if it's on sale for $20 - which probably won't happen for a long time.
good game. could use more acurate outfits and stages + different anime dub scores

The actual game play is fine. But that’s pretty much the only good thing. They tricked people by saying there are 100+ characters when it’s really closer to 50 with transformations. They haven’t added ANYTHING to the store. What’s the point of making outfit slots on each character if only Goku and vegeta get outfits. At least the old games had inverted colored skins for each character. The story mode sucked and honestly, vegeta sounds like he was voiced by AI. And it was like they couldn’t decide which cutscenes should have voice overs and which should just be dead silent and make you read it all. Budakai tenkaichi 3 was %1000 better. You can’t do an unlimited time match with your friends online. The unlockables are basically calling cards that no one cares about. I’ve been very disappointed with dragon ball. Has no grit, has no story, has no respect for the fans. They know they have you, so they don’t put in the effort. And they are making ANOTHER battle of gods movie… the first one was GREAT. We didn’t need it reanimated in super tv show. And we don’t need THE SAME MOVIE AGAIN. And the gohan movie.. sucked.. copy and pasted the cell saga but make it worse. Completely disappointed in DB.

Good game to pick up on sale, 200+ characters is nice, and I had a lot of fun doing all steam achievements

I just wish the online had rollback, and crossplay. I can't play with most of my friends who have this game, and the input delay is miserable. It makes consistently vanishing not something you can really do online. I know the focus is the roster + the story, but this game has a massive staff and massive budget, they can add these features, and while I don't believe they will, I hope they consider. FighterZ has rollback, I don't see why this can't.

It'll scratch that itch for all of the old-school Budokai Tenkaichi fans, though would recommend waiting until the game is on sale to really get your money's worth (how many Gokus does a game need?) 

Yeah they're adding a lot of the missing fan favorites in future DLC and there's already quite a few characters under the hood, but some of the story mission What-Ifs have cryptic alternate path requirements and some battles feel clunky since they built the story missions with the same system that the player can create missions with (which has CLASSIC Japanese UI design btw, so I hope you have half an hour to manually sift through more pages of singular nouns than English homework when you're creating a mission). 

Also, why not store Dragon Balls for later instead of deleting them if you already have a full set of the same kind? Between that, the UI, and the horse armor-ass death-by-a-thousand-cuts DLC (especially the cosmetics and buffs) , I'd give this game a 7.5/10. Most Dragon Ball fans will want this, but $100 for all the fixins when the game could use a few more patches and some interface QOL updates? Hell naw. I can go get in a street fight with a guy that calls me a monkey for free.
The combat system is too complicated for this to be fun for casuals. Makes me miss the old Tenkaichi games.

I played and loved the budokai series. This game looked like the spiritual successor.
It is not.
The gameplay is incredibly shallow, character customization is almost nonexistent. All the characters feel basically identical, except they look different from one another.

I'd skip this.
game feels like getting shot in the spine as a dragon ball fan. I didnt even review this game on release cause of how disappointed I was

The cutscenes feel and look like lazy power points, balance online isnt good, there still isnt a lot of content in this game even compare to the previous Tenkaichi games especially for the price, you have to rely on the game to lock in on an enemy for you which can take too long, too easy too cheese and spam especially with super perception.
need crossplay... so hard to find opponent.... waste time just on setting

I'm very disappointed with this game. This was supposed to be the spiritual successor to the Budokai Tenkaichi series which I played as a kid. Unfortunately the gameplay feels clunky and laggy. Something's just not there. If you're an adult and want to play this game for nostalgia, wait to get it on sale. It's definitely not worth the full price.

some kind of bug keeps popping up idk what its called but the enemy gets 1hp and is unkillable and it spreads to the modded characters and you cant play as them because if u play the character its one life and done if you get hit its annoying to go against the characters and hard to play as your favorite character.
I guess it's alright. The motion captured animations do ruin the immersion of its style.

its a really good game, but i haven't seen cpu input reading this bad since budokai 1.. if you have no problem with it thats awesome for you but doesn't deflate it as an issue....

The ai especially on harder diffs will just counter every 2nd or 3rd attack pulling off frame perfect counters constantly throughout the match. also not sure why we changed vanishing to block and circle/B instead of just circle/B... lot less comfortable to pull off.

honesltly fix the ai (not easy to do) don't make the ai branidead but maybe tune them down a bit when it comes to the z counters

This is the most annoying game I've ever played in my ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ life. "Normal" difficulty feels impossible, even as someone who played the ♥♥♥♥ out of the old ones on my ps2, and lowering the difficulty is too easy. Enemies immediately use their special abilities when the match starts and lock you into an inescapable state of being rapidly pulverized, leaving zero room for learning the fights, and often spam ki abilities. Attacks are too fast to counter and the teleport dodge thing only works half the time. It also never explains a lot of the combat ♥♥♥♥ like the quick time events so you're left to figure ♥♥♥♥ out on your own in the middle of fights that are already insanely over tuned. This game is built to piss you off.

 This is NOT fun.
Un juego muy bueno con un platino que se goza de principio a fin recomendado para jugador completista como casual...
If you loved playing the Tenkaichi series back in the day, then this game is definitely for you. So much fun! Highly recommended
The AI can and will 100% perfectly block, dodge, or counter every single attack regardless of difficulty. Genuinely the worst fighting game AI I have every encountered.

I've never been great at the BT series, I always played on a more casual friendly difficulty but I enjoyed it all the same. I replayed the series for the release of Sparking Zero but didn't actually have the funds to get this game until recently. It was on sale, I thought perfect, I've wanted to play this for a while! 

My main issue is actually with the difficulty. Sure sure "get gud" or whatever, but that's not what I'm frustrated about. I understand the game is hard, but there are certain timing based mechanics where you just need to predict, and you will always be slower than the Ai, they have your inputs and just counter you easily. Sure there's cheese strats, but I feel like that kind of defeats the fun of the game. My real issue comes with when you lower the difficulty in story mode, you lose out on literally all the drops. Why? Like why? What good does it serve to gatekeep half the content by a skill barrier that 90% of people who play this game can't surpass? I don't even want to play online, I just want to feel like an achievement go getter and play the game that way, but I literally can't. Can you beat Kid Buu without some cheese strat?? Can you beat 85% of the fights in this game without spamming some broken pattern like Beerus did against me??? 

Listen the nostalgia is great, it reminds me a lot of the BT series, but something is genuinely missing. I'm not even really sure what. Maybe a moderate difficulty option? Maybe just give people the stuff they deserve for buying the game and progressing?? Was this like an online safeguard? To prevent people from getting really late game content early?? I'm genuinely confused as to what the thought process behind this was. Also the game doesn't really feel content heavy. I'd love to play the more what-if scenarios, because those were my favorite in the older series, but WHERE ARE THEY??? If they're hidden behind "normal difficulty" unlocks I swear I'll crash out. How do you allow this to happen? Half the game is a powerpoint presentation. What was wrong with just changing perspectives from character to character. That was okay!! Even if sometimes it was drawn on a little too long (lookin at you Hercule vs Android 18). I just don't know why the dark souls craze of ridiculously difficult=fun. Because it doesn't. We're so hellbent on trying to make rage games now a days that we've sort of lost the best part about this series. Reliving your childhood memories of dragonball in a different medium. 

I'll still play obviously, but I'm just disappointed. I'm completing it for the past BT's sakes, but I'm upset. Maybe no one cares, but if you genuinely enjoyed the Budokai Tenkaichi series and you don't see anything wrong with what we were delivered, I'm just confused. What about the og series did you enjoy that they captured here because it just feels like mechs on mechs with little to no opportunity for you to actually play. I got combo locked by Ai for a solid 10-15 seconds. They block my combo startup in a matter of micro seconds. I'm already burnt out and it's been less than 4 hours of playtime.

if you played budokai tenkaichi 2 on the ps2 back then, this feels exactly like it. Although the story feels a bit meh and really quick, the gameplay is pretty much what you had back then with a bit more characters. A lot of buttom mashing, really quick fights and a bunch of action.

Unfortunately FPS are capped at 60, but other than that, no issues at all.
Great game! Specially if you are nostalgic about the original Budokai Tenkaichi series.

game has a boring half azzed story mode that has absolutely zero love put into it they boast about a gigantic roster yet over half of them are just goku and vegeta done 20 times over with nothing more than a different beam or rush attack here and there if you count only the fighters themselves and not al of their transformations you cut the roster by more than half and its pathetic they lied to us and played on our nostalgia and loyalty to rip us off and then sell packs with people that should have been in from the start we got one extra game mode coming and its a garbage attempt at an rpg spinoff that no-one wanted or asked for instead of actually making the game fun and adding different modes or redoing the story mode and adding morer people to play as wouldnt even recommend on sale to be honest just play fighterz or kakarot at this point
Best fighting game in this genre for people who want to play with friends.

If you grew up playing the Budokai Tenkaichi series, it's a true return to form. Bots are enjoyably difficult, and the online community, while it has its stinkers still, seems to be here for fun fights and competitive comradery.

one of the worst games I've ever had the enjoyment of playing. I would rather play rust with my toes and my headset on backwards for 10 consecutive years than to play it. 1,000,000/10 game though I absolutely loved it.
It's genuine fun. I'm not good/like fighting games, but I love Dragon Ball so I push through that aspect. My favorite part is making custom battles, although it can be very frustrating at times.

Honnestly i couldnt ask for a more peak dragon ball game other than dragon ball fighterz,
PEAK.

The game has a lot of mechanics for positioning yourself for maximum effect, the sheer number of characters with the different supers changes playstyles fairly well, while still having the core be understandable. with the DP limits, it allows for a versatility of builds with restrictions, yet its still viable for different builds to be useful. the big downside is the walls. characters pushed against them seem to have an unfair advantage of recovery and attack, and sometimes missing sure hits on opponents against the wall. great game, I give an 8 of 10 with the new survival mode update.

its good game but hard to master , you need to be hawk to perry attacks and the storys of what IF is good but i hate the short storys like when goku killed cell the ending only shown nothing more, this game is good overall
literally a dead game, cannot get a match. waste of my ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ money

Ive been wanted this game for a while and had to take advantage on the deal. 
Game mechanic is definetly challenging, it takes some time getting used to it. 
Im more into single player. It is refreshing to get alternate stories and the game doesnt spend to much time in main story explanation because any fan likes it. It is also cool, how you progress through the story in the eyes of the character you choose.
Need to get more into the hang of playing before trying out the mutliplayer.

Good game but I randomly got this glitch witch makes the crash on start up so I haven't played it in a while. But to the people considering to buy the game give it a try especially because of the update adding new stuff. However don't buy the season pass it isn't worth it.
Why is this fighting game a fighting game? ♥♥♥♥♥ game, dont fall for it. Dont buy, dont sell, dont play, dont do anything about this game, were done here next.

i was wrong, i didnt want multiplayer kakarot with more cheese supplementing the interactive combat buttons like how xenoverse feels. i feel like im just playing a button and timing spreasheet simulator, not like im goku/dnz john/any other of the 30 gokus.
I like the game but the lag when I use keyboard and mouse is extremely irritateing.
fix ur damn game what is wrong with you folks charging a high price with so many bugs and glitches. if your game ain't ready dont' release garbage, stupid devs

I liked how the game looks. With its animations, its visuals, it's good.

BUT GOOD LORD. I'm so tired by the time i get to the majin buu arc. It's like I'm banging my head to a brick wall screaming "WHAT AM I DOING WRONG???" The cpu gets too advanced for me to keep up. I tried going into tutorial and understand recoveries and counters. But they're so INCONSISTENT!! I'm not trying to be a TRYHARD AT THIS GAME. I'm playing casually, yet im getting punished for it?!

I don't mind challenges. I truly don't. But HOLY CRAP. I've been going back and forth. Looking at what I'm doing wrong. Trying to adapt. IT'S JUST TOO FAST AND THE TIMING IS TOO TIGHT! I tried making use of super armor, I tried different perspectives. But there is so much to learn, so much to do, It's like I'm looking at a Tekken Character Moveset Combo Sheet! Of course, unfairly comparable. But the exaggeration is there!

I don't care if it's a skill issue, I genuinely can't enjoy this game with its story mode if the CPU is 10 steps ahead of me while I'm trying to adapt and use what I got. Every time I think of a move to do, SUPER ARMOR or VANISHING BEHIND ME. And it's too fast for me to react to! Or like... Too fast that it makes me feel like that I can't do anything! Attack, Charge attack, throwing random special moves, the CPU reacts! I can't!

Functionally, it is a fine game, I can't complain. The skill increase in the buu saga is just insane. It puts me off from playing the game while I'm left to figure out the muscle memory and techniques all by myself. I could be lazy. I could be unmotivated. But every time I try to play, it's just. Not. Fun.

This game makes me dislike/hate Majin Buu and his variants. What an annoying character.

Multiplayer sucks and AI in this game sucks - reading inputs doesn't mean harder difficulty. 

They took the Sparking series and ♥♥♥♥♥♥ it like every other game that's come out in the past 6-7 years. Plenty of larping content - and garbage ass story.
Cool looking dudes having cool looking fights in cool looking places. Easy 10/10
lags beyond words good job ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ up your game if you dont fix it by next update gonna seek a refund and i garentee you steam will give it too me

Pros: dragonball

Cons: Ai sucks, players suck, call it a skill issue but I dont wanna get railed up the butt by some guy using zarbon, living in his basement, eating tostitos he found between his fatrolls as he grooms children on roblox using his second moniter, whilst he daydreams of being some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ like a professional ♥♥♥♥ cleaner once he finally gets out of his stepmoms basment and goes to ♥♥♥♥ cleaning college. and thats WHEN it the multiplayer works. Most of the time your just left there staring at your empty pubic lobby while reflecting on how much money youve wasted to see virtual anime guys fight.

overall 10/10, Makes me feel proud about where I am in life everytime I play pvp.
at 1st it was rly good but ever since the online player base was dieing they just made such bad descisions like making every character have the same stats

Worst game ever, waste of time and money. This game is only good for nostalgia blinded fans who cant tell the difference between a good game and a mediocre game. I'm going back to the BT4 mod of BT3. This is the difference between passion and money. Game can't even add characters like SS1 Bardock, GT Goten, etc even after 30 years and even when mods have those characters free and ready. Customization f-ing sucks. Just look at their new DLC. Beam struggles SUCK, why the hell are the beams so damn small and tiny? They fire humungous beams of energy in a 20 plus year old game and cant fire a large beam in this "modern" pathetic excuse for a videogame!

I waited almost 20 years for a sequel to Tenkaichi 3 and we have it. It's not a perfect game, but it's what I wanted. If you enjoy Tenkaichi series and just throwing characters against one another like action figures, you're gonna enjoy it

Though this game has not kept to the promises it had at launch it's a good game you'll like it if you like Dragon ball or Arena fighting games one of the best for sure, but being a huge fan who bought a terribly priced early version of this game it was not a good consumer experience.


The promised DLCs were bare and never on time.

Theres like a whole unused alternative costumes thing that only like 3 characters even use

Very few maps and most of them were in the last installment nothing new or creative.

Wait for a sale, this is not the Budokai Tenkaichi that we had back then, it's decent but very lackluster and the mechanics have changed, very evidently so.

Still, it's a decent arena fighter, just not worth the initial asking price.
this game has ruined my life, i am currently failing all my classes and this stupid game because every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ match is fully unfair i hate this game and you pay with your wallet and soul.

If youre like me, you grew up with the sparking series as a kid, and one of my favorite memories was searching up videos of modded BT3. Sparking zero isnt one of the best in the series but it does offer the ability to have a title in the budokai tenkaichi series thats easily moddable. 

10/10 personally, worth it if youre a big fan of the anime, massively worth it if youre a modder junkie like me
